phrases.intent_en: Ask about the ferry to the islands
phrases.text_local: Comment aller aux îles du Frioul ?
phrases.pronunciation: kuh-MAN ah-LAY oh EEL doo free-OOL
phrases.cultural_note: Ferries run every 30 minutes from the Vieux-Port. The 15-minute crossing offers views of Marseille and Château d'If.

responses.english: Take the ferry at the Vieux-Port, every thirty minutes.
responses.phonetic: toot lay kah-NEEZ ah vahn mee-NOOT
responses.french: Prenez le ferry au Vieux-Port, toutes les trente minutes.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il des plages sur les îles ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL day PLAHZH soor layz EEL
phrases.cultural_note: The islands have small beaches, but they can get crowded in summer. The water is crystal clear.

responses.english: Yes, there are small beaches. The water is very clear.
responses.french: Oui, il y a de petites plages. L'eau est très claire.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the ferry crossing takes
phrases.text_local: Combien de temps dure la traversée ?
phrases.pronunciation: kom-BYAN duh tahn DOOR lah trah-vair-SAY
phrases.cultural_note: The ferry crossing from Vieux-Port to the Frioul islands is quick and scenic.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un restaurant sur les îles ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL uhn res-to-RAN soor layz EEL
phrases.cultural_note: There's one restaurant on Ratonneau island. Bring a picnic for the best experience — there are few options.

responses.english: Yes, just one on the island. Bring a picnic.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ee ahn plu-ZYUR dahn luh kar-TYAY
responses.french: Oui, un seul sur l'île. Prévoyez un pique-nique.
